subject: Re: Adding a species in M from relion/ Is there a log file ?
body:
Hi Josh,

Your star file needs to point to the particles in on your windows
partition. Also, you need to append .tomostar to the end of micrograph
name.

eg. mic1 now becomes mic1.tomostar

If you want to look for an error log this txt file generally sits in
the same folder in which the warp exe sits.
Best,
Pranav
--
Pranav Shah
Postdoctoral Research Fellow.

Division of Structural Biology,
Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
University of Oxford,
Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
UK

Best,
Pranav
--
Pranav Shah
Postdoctoral Research Fellow.

Division of Structural Biology,
Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
University of Oxford,
Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
UK

On Fri, Aug 28, 2020 at 2:52 PM Joshua Lobo <joshua.jude.lobo@gmail.com> wrote:
>
> Hi Warp Group
>
> I am trying to import a run_data.star from a relion 3.0 single particle refinement  directly into M . I follow the steps to add a new spices and they execute  without a hitch . The import of the star file is also seemingly successful . However I get a message as seen in the screenshot (0/Number of particles matched to available data sources)
>
> I was wondering if there is a log file for M where any particular import errors are dumped or a debug mode so I can figure if M is parsing my file correctly
>
> Thanks
> Josh
>
> --
